data.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c-cbcd-17a0-43d2 (plan-review fan-out optimization). Reviewed THIS child (S2 — engine-wide caching via RunnerAgentStep) WHOLE against the complete sibling roster (S1 0250, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes. NO G4 FINDINGS.

Affirmations:
- Implicit shared state / consumer impact: S2 consumes S1's shared-runner cache mechanism (anthropic_cache_instructions, runner.py:421-435) and extends it to the engine RunnerAgentStep (workflow/runs.py:207 build_agent_request). Producer→consumer is DAG-ordered (S1->S2 dep link present on 0250). Shared-file split is clean: S1 owns runner.py (cache-flag mechanism); S2 owns workflow/runs.py + prompts.py (RunnerAgentStep prefix-wiring + the stable-vs-volatile prompt-model convention + volatile-data relocation). S1's own G4 review reciprocally affirms this split.
- Conflicting assumptions: S2's case is correctly DISTINCT from S1's, not contradictory. S1 caches the container system_prompt directly because the {{plan}} prefix is already byte-stable; S2's workflow system prompt is NOT byte-stable (interpolates per-run ticket_id/ticket_context at workflow/runs.py:131-146 resolve_prompt), so S2's MAJOR-6 remediation correctly adds the relocation step S1 did not need. S2's de-risk E3 comment explicitly reconciles the two domains (stable container {{plan}} = S1; volatile gate-prompt {{ticket_context}} = S2). Consistent with the parent's "cache the SYSTEM PROMPT … no RunRequest content-list change" remediation.
- Dependency gap / ordering: S2 depends only on S1 (link present); it sits in the fidelity-neutral tranche (S1,S3,S2 ship ahead of eval-gated S4/S5). No eval-gate ordering obligation, no gap. The strict S3->S4->S5 sizing.py serialization does not touch S2 (S2 edits workflow/runs.py + prompts.py + parity.py, not pass1.py/sizing.py).
- Scope overlap: S2 fences out S1 (runner mechanism) and S3 (parallelization); S1 reciprocally fences out RunnerAgentStep caching ("Out: engine RunnerAgentStep caching (S2)"). No file overlap with the container path (S3/S4/S5 = pass1.py/sizing.py).
- Ordering violation: none — S2 is correctly downstream of S1 and parallel-independent of the container chain.
- Residual references: no sibling carries a contract against a design S2 removes. The relocation S2 performs is reconciled against S1 (de-risk E3) and against S6 (S2's G3-1/G4-A fidelity-verify AC reuses parity.py).
- Consumer-impact note already CLOSED (not a finding): S2 relocates per-run data across 11 workflow prompts including the completion-verifier that backs THIS project's mandatory close gate (and code-review). The single-pass G3/G4 remediation (G3-1/G4-A) already converted the bare "no finding change" assertion into a FIDELITY-VERIFIED AC (reuse parity.py recall/false-accept or a targeted spot eval). Self-remediated ⇒ not a live G4 finding.

Non-G4 editorial note (intra-child, below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold; benign under ANTI-FP): the S2 body still uses the old epic-era "cacheable-prefix boundary … after the CachePoint / a separate message part" framing, which the parent's S1 remediation and S2's own MAJOR-6 relocation supersede (operative mechanism: move volatile ticket data to the USER message + mark the stable/volatile split, then anthropic_cache_instructions over the now-stable system prefix). Stale terminology within one child, not a cross-child inconsistency — recorded for editorial cleanup only.
